slides.title: Nature and the Environment
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 3 English, Unit 7: Nature and the Environment. Welcome to Unit 7! In this unit you will explore the natural world — the weather and the climate, the beach and the coast, forests, mountains, and even our planet Earth. You will learn to talk about places you have visited using a new tense called the present perfect (“I have explored a forest”). You will describe nature with colourful words, ask good questions, and learn how to care for the environment. By the end, you will be ready to go on a nature adventure of your own, just like Adam and Amal in this unit's story. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name and describe places in nature: the beach, coast, forest, and mountain
Talk about the weather, the climate, and the temperature
Say what you have done using the present perfect: “I have seen the sea.”
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name and describe places in nature: the beach, coast, forest, and mountain. you will also talk about the weather, the climate, and the temperature. you will also say what you have done using the present perfect: “i have seen the sea.”. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: climate, weather, temperature, sunshine, froze, mountain. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Our Wonderful Nature and A Poem: “Nature Is Our Home”. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: The Present Perfect Tense
bullets: We use the present perfect to talk about something that happened in the past but still matters now. When you say “I have explored a forest,” it shows this...
slides.narration: The first language focus is The Present Perfect Tense. We use the present perfect to talk about something that happened in the past but still matters now. When you say “I have explored a forest,” it shows this is part of who you are today. It helps you share your experiences with others. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Speaking 1 — Describe a Place in Nature
Writing 1 — Describe the Nature Near You
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 — Describe a Place in Nature. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
